summary: chai-sdk fetches and executes remote code from anonymous host on module use
details: chai-sdk@1.4.7 impersonates the pino/chai ecosystems (README rebranded from pino, exports mimicking pino middleware) as a lure. index.js defines middleware that spawns a detached, stdio-ignored `node lib/initializeCaller.js` child process (via child_process.spawn with detached:true and child.unref()) so the payload runs independently of the caller. lib/initializeCaller.js POSTs to a base64-obscured URL (decoded to https://amethyst-lorrin-26.tiiny.site/index.json) and passes the response.data.cookie field to `new Function('require',...)` invoked with the real require, granting the remote-hosted code arbitrary execution against the installer with full Node module access. The destination is an anonymous, mutable file-hosting site (tiiny.site), the URL is hidden behind atob() to defeat static inspection, and there is no pinning, hash, or signature check on the fetched code. The combination of typosquat lure, obfuscated destination, stealth-launch child process, and remote-fetch-and-exec into `new Function(require,...)` is an unambiguous dropper.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
